reviews.review_msg: AMAZING PROF HE ROUNDED ME FROM AN A- TO AN A. But seriously tho his lecture videos were hilarious and was never bored watching them!!

ROUNDED ME UP BLESS HIM didn't even need to ask and he rounded me up such a good professor and wish ever professor would boost you up if it is just ONE PERCENT!! ANYWAYS TAKE HIM SUCH A GPA BOOSTER CLASS

Bless this man's soul, rounded me up from an A- to A. 

Class is interesting and informative! 10/10 recommend.
Prof is amazing he rounded all my friends up to an A or A+. HE IS THE GOAT

This course content itself wasn't too bad, but as a condensed course INSANE. Get ready to write down every single detail in the slides, and that he says in the lectures, and you'll at least get a B+ on open book exams. 33% for 3 exams, 60 mins for 60 questions. If youre ready to put in the work can be a GPA booster.

60 minute 60 question test worth 33% of your grade, his lectures are fine and the exam is open book yet it's insane when you have slow wifi canvas takes forever to refresh which crunches on the time. Questions are designed to get you through misreading, which is common when you literally almost less than a minute per question. Grading is messed up.

Lectures are simple slides. 90% of the grade was split amongst 5 different quizzes and the extra 10% was basically a participation mark for the discussions. It is possible to do entire quizzes to a satisfactory level by Ctrl+F'ing the lecture slides while doing the quiz without looking at them beforehand. Excellent GPA booster/stabilizer.

Amazing prof! Took 2 courses with him and actually enjoyed both. GPA booster on God - but only if you actually study a bit and don't just ChatGPT your way through. Content was super interesting, and grading is straightforward: 5 quizzes, 20% each. Just put in some effort, and you're golden. Highly recommend!

David Maxwell is chill, and his lectures are surprisingly interesting—which is impressive considering it's archaeology. But he makes it engaging, especially with his lecture recordings. The course? Easy - 5 quizzes (carry your grade) and 2 assignments (dropped mine faster than a bad stock pick). Overall: fun, doable, don't mess up the assignments!

The online class content is very organized and understandable. I took another online class with him, and it was so good that I took it! Lectures are pretty engaging he has a nice voice and tone to his teaching.
The marking is mainly just from online quizzes/ tests and the courseload is pretty light.

ARCH226 is an interesting online class with a fair grading system. While the lectures can be lengthy, they are well-structured. To succeed, take notes, use unstuckstudy for study prep, and stay engaged. Workload is manageable with some effort.

This class is so amazing! Its got super interesting course content, recorded lectures can be a bit tedious to get through. If you set aside an hour or two a week, you can get through the course easily. Graded 90% on module quizzes, 10% on discussion posts. Honestly pretty easy quizzes if you actually understand the lectures. Would recommend!

100% of your grade comes from 3 online exams. There is a lot of information to memorize.  It was very difficult for me. While exams are open book, answering 60 questions in 60 minutes is simply not enough time. If you are good at remembering take the course, otherwise try to avoid.

The class had only 2 midterms and a final. The questions were extremely nit-picky (like what was significant about *year*). I took this as an elective and it was not a GPA booster. The lecture slides are disorganized and the insignificant material that is barely mentioned will be on the midterms and final.

4000+ words for each lecture.. for real somebody needs to teach him how to present information properly...he literally will use slides that have 200+ words on each slide. Most information is useless but you wont know until after the quizzes so get it all down! If you are not interested in rocks then this may be the most boring class you take
Definitely do not take him online, reads off lectures and has no scaling even though the quiz averages are bad. Quizzes are extremely hard and barely any time to finish them.

The marking for this class was relatively fair and post midterm topics were interesting. However, if you are looking for a class with little weekly work, this might not be the class for you. Weekly lectures could range upwards of 7 different videos, and each video was between 20-40 minutes. Need to put in work to do well for sure. Prof was nice
One of the worst electives I have taken, advice: never take any courses taught by this prof
The class is not worth taking at all, in fact, avoid taking any classes with this professor.

A great professor.. for in person classes. It's hours of videos that are just him reading directly off slides that have a massive chunk of text on them that look like they were pasted in from a textbook. The content is very interesting sometimes and dreadfully boring at others. Assignments/exams are very manageable, but lectures are not.
